name: quality_constellation_v1
description: Curated output-quality benchmark for comparing expert constellations on N04-RTX. Covers core experts (math, code_reviewer, reasoning, science) and four newly invented experts (data_analysis, creative_writing, devops_sre, security_analysis). Each case is judge-scored (0-10) plus deterministic keyword/numeric checks. Sovereign-only.
author: constellation-experiment

test_cases.name: Compound interest (monthly)
test_cases.prompt: Compute exactly: a principal of 10000 EUR at 4.5% nominal annual interest, compounded monthly, after 3 years. Give the final amount rounded to 2 decimals and show the formula.

test_cases.name: Spot the SQL injection
Mislabeled boxes logic puzzle
test_cases.prompt: Three boxes are labeled 'Apples', 'Oranges', and 'Mixed'. You are told every label is wrong. You may draw exactly one fruit from exactly one box. From which box do you draw, and explain how that single draw lets you correctly relabel all three boxes.

expected_answer.reason: since all labels are wrong, 'Mixed' is pure; the drawn fruit reveals its true content, the rest follow by elimination

test_cases.name: Blue sky and red sunset (Rayleigh)
test_cases.prompt: Explain why the daytime sky is blue but sunsets appear red. Ground your explanation in Rayleigh scattering and the wavelength dependence of scattering.

expected_answer.mechanism: Rayleigh scattering ~ 1/lambda^4; short (blue) wavelengths scatter more; at sunset light travels a longer path so blue is scattered out, leaving red

test_cases.name: Pandas: sales per region
test_cases.prompt: You have a pandas DataFrame `df` with columns 'region' (string) and 'sales' (float). Write Python code to compute total sales per region, sort descending, and print the top region. Briefly explain each step.

test_cases.name: Outlier, median vs mean robustness
test_cases.prompt: Given the values [12, 15, 11, 14, 13, 100]: identify the outlier, report the median and the mean (rounded to 1 decimal), and state which measure is more robust to the outlier and why.

test_cases.name: Constrained lighthouse poem
test_cases.prompt: Write a 4-line poem about a lighthouse. It must contain the words 'storm', 'beam', and 'silent', stay under 50 words, and have a consistent rhythm. Output only the poem.

expected_answer.constraints: 4 lines; contains storm, beam, silent; <50 words; coherent imagery

test_cases.name: Diagnose CrashLoopBackOff
test_cases.prompt: A Kubernetes pod is stuck in CrashLoopBackOff. Give a systematic, ordered diagnostic procedure to find the root cause, naming the concrete kubectl commands you would run at each step.

expected_answer.steps: kubectl describe pod (events), kubectl logs --previous, check exit code/OOMKilled, liveness/readiness probes, resource limits, config/secret/mounts

test_cases.name: Dockerfile best-practice review
test_cases.prompt:
Review this Dockerfile and name at least three concrete problems with fixes:

FROM ubuntu:latest
RUN apt-get install -y python3
COPY . /app
CMD python3 /app/main.py

(Note it runs as root and does not pin versions.)

expected_answer.issues: unpinned 'latest' tag; missing apt-get update + no-install-recommends + cache cleanup; runs as root (add non-root USER); no WORKDIR; broad COPY busts cache; no slim base

test_cases.name: MD5 password storage risks
test_cases.prompt: A web application stores user passwords hashed with MD5. Explain the concrete security risks and describe the correct modern approach to password storage.

expected_answer.risks: MD5 fast + broken (collisions), no salt -> rainbow tables, brute-force
expected_answer.correct: slow salted KDF: bcrypt/scrypt/argon2 with per-user salt

test_cases.name: Identify the XSS vulnerability
test_cases.prompt: In a web app, user-supplied input is rendered directly into an HTML page without any escaping. Name the vulnerability class precisely, explain the attack, and give the correct mitigation(s).

expected_answer.mitigation: context-aware output encoding/escaping, input validation, Content-Security-Policy
